Notice Of Joint Motion And Joint Motion To Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's motion to tax costs is granted in its entirety. All costs claimed by plaintiff Berkley Assurance Company against Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor are taxed. Assuming without deciding that the motion was not timely filed per CRC 3.1700(b)(1), the court has authority per CRC 3.1700(b)(3) to extend the time for filing the motion by 30 days and does so here so that the motion is adjudicated on its merits. Per CCP 1032(a)(4), the court exercises its discretion to tax all claimed costs as to Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor for three reasons: 1) all or nearly all of those claimed costs would have been incurred as to other defendants whether or not Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor were named as defendants in this case; 2) this case was primarily against other defendants, not Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor; and 3) Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor appear to be the victims of other defendants' tortious behavior and, as a result of the court's rulings in this case, their losses will not be covered by the other defendants' insurance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ashley W. Nagashima, Esq. of Walsh McKean Furcolo LLP, T:619.232.8486, for plaintiff/moving party;
Robert V. Closson, Esq. of Hirsch Closson APLC, T:619.233.7006 ext.224, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF BERKLEY ASSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication as follows:

Plaintiff Berkley Assurance Company's motion for summary judgment is granted and it is entitled to a judgment rescinding the policy of insurance it issued to the Law Offices of David Wiseblood and for damages in its favor in the amount of $57,459.12 against defendants David Wiseblood and Law Offices of David Wiseblood. The court previously determined that Berkley is entitled to rescind the policy it issued to the Law Offices of David Wiseblood, but left open the issue of whether Berkley was entitled to recover any of the fees and costs it incurred in defending the Wiseblood defendants in the underlying action. The court now determines that, per LA Sound USA. Inc. v. St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Co. (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 1259, Berkley is entitled to recover the full amount of $57,459.12 it sought in this motion since it is undisputed that Berkely paid that amount to defend the Wiseblood defendants in the underlying action. Per LA Sound, Berkley is entitled to recover all of the fees and costs it paid to defend the Wiseblood defendants as a result of the rescission of the policy for which a defense was provided. Berkley's request for an additional award of $35,569.14 may not properly be considered on this motion because no portion of that amount was included in the moving papers. However, if it chooses to do so, as an exception to the stay previously imposed in this case, Berkley is permitted to file a supplemental complaint and a new motion for summary judgment/adjudication limited to the issue of its entitlement to recover any additional monies it claims to have spent in defense of the Wiseblood defendants in the underlying case in excess of $57,459.12.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.

Hearing Re Defendant'S Objections To Plaintiff'S Evidence In Support Of The Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Landers Niven, Esq. Hirsch Closson, Tel. 925.935.9800, for defendants and moving party;
Ashley Nagashima, Esq. of Walsh McKean Furcolo, LLP, Tel. 619.232.8486, for plaintiffs;
Leon Taylor, defendant, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on Defendant'S Objections To Plaintiff'S Evidence In Support Of The Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication, as follows: Defendants' objections to the admissibility of the declaration of David Wiseblood filed on October 29, 2015 in the Migdal case are sustained. The reasoning of the alternative holding in Smith v. Lewis (1975) 13 Cal. 3d 349, 364-65 excluding the CCP 473 declaration by counsel in that case on "extrinsic policy" grounds apply equally to a lawsuit such as this one alleging the absence of attorney malpractice insurance coverage as it does to a lawsuit such as Smith alleging the existence of attorney malpractice. The use of the word "Furthermore" to begin a new paragraph after the paragraph in Smith discussing Evidence Code 352 and the language of the paragraph that begins with the word "Furthermore" strongly suggests that Smith held that the attorney declaration in that case was inadmissible on both section 352 and "extrinsic policy" grounds. While section 352 is arguably inapplicable to a motion for summary judgment (People ex rel. City of Dana Point v. Holistic Health (2013) 213 Cal. App. 4th 1016, 1029), extrinsic policy grounds for the inadmissibility of evidence are applicable to a motion for summary judgment. (See, e.g., McKee v. State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. (1983) 145 Cal. App. 3d 772, 777-78). Defendants' objections to the declarations of Dale Diamond and Tammy Garland are overruled. There is sufficient foundation for each of the statements in those declarations that have been objected to and the statements are not inadmissible for lack of personal knowledge, impermissible opinion, or hearsay. Defendant David Wiseblood's objections to the pleadings, motions and other papers filed by Mr. Midgal and orders issued in the Migdal case are sustained as to the truth of the allegations and statements in those documents, but overruled as to the fact that such allegations and statements were made. Mr. Wiseblood's objections to the first amended complaint and proof of service filed in the RAF case, the complaint filed by Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor in the underlying malpractice case and the complaint filed in this case are also sustained as to the truth of the allegations and statements in those documents, but overruled as to the fact that such allegations and statements were made. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
